organizations.role: Founder & principal (political research and crisis communications firm)
organizations.notes: Well-known Austin figure. Longtime Democratic political consultant/strategist, author (co-author of 'Forget the Alamo'), former communications director for Mayor Steve Adler; since April 2021 the Chief of Communications and Community Engagement at Austin ISD. Zip 78751 and AISD employer match the public record.

Well-known Austin figure. organizations.role: Tax Assessor-Collector and county voter registrar (former)
organizations.notes: Travis County Archives' elections exhibit documents Cecelia Burke as Travis County Tax Assessor-Collector (pictured at a 1989 voter registration drive); she preceded Bruce Elfant, who took office in 2013. The donor's Austin 78731 residence and extremely heavy, near-uniformly Democratic federal giving pattern (533 contributions, partisan lean 0.999) are consistent with a retired countywide Democratic officeholder, but no source directly ties the 78731 donor record to the former officeholder, so identity is probable rather than confirmed. No TEC lobbyist registration; no PAC or advocacy-org tie on any side of the searched spectrums.

organizations.role: Former Chair of the Board of Directors
organizations.notes: Robin Rather, CEO of Austin-based sustainability consultancy Collective Strength, longtime Austin environmental activist and daughter of newsman Dan Rather. Occupation 'Consultant' / employer 'Self-employed' and zip 78704 match her published FEC contributor records (Robin Rather, CEO, Collective Strength, Austin 78704). Prior career as a tech-sector market research executive at IntelliQuest, CMP Media, and as CEO of Mindwave Research; ~25 years of market research and strategic planning work on 50+ climate, land conservation, water, transit, and regional planning projects. Her all-Democratic federal giving pattern (122 FEC records, lean 1.0) is consistent with her public profile.
